metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_2392
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Postsynaptic PAK1 stimulates presynaptic GABA release by inhibiting negative feedback of endocannabinoids.
Fig1: PAK1 null has impaired E/I balance (Fig1C), mainly through impaired IPSC input on CA1 pyramidal neurons, which is also mimicked by PAK1 inhibitor or microdialysis of inhibiting peptide in the pyramidal neuron (post synaptic 1F). Fig 2 and 3 show that mIPSC frequency is impaired in the PAK1 null or PAK inhibitor, amplitude is normal. mEPSCs are also normal, indicating that presynaptic GABA release is selectively impaired. This is verified in Fig4G that shows a GABA puff on cultured PAK1 null neurons gives a normal postsynaptic response. Fig 6 shows that blocking postsynaptic PAK1 by KO, inhibitor or peptide these all enhance CB1 antagonist effect, indicating enhanced endocannabinoid signalling in absence of postsynaptic PAK1 leads to a reduction of presynaptic GABA release. This enhancement of the CB1 antagonist is phenocopied by COX-2 inhibition in WT cells, and no further enhancement is observed in the PAK1 null (Fig8B) indicating that PAK1 inhibits retrograde endocannabinoid signalling through COX-2. In line with this, less COX-2 is observed in synapses of PAK1 null mice (Fig7K, 9M).. Experimental description: M&M: "All the mice used in this study were PAK1 KO (Pak1-/-) offspring and their wild type littermaze (Pak1+/+) derived PAK1 heterozygous (Pak1+/-) breeders to minimize the effect of genetic or environmental factors."
